message:
refactor(showcase,wasm): extract pane interaction logic into shared module

Extract PanePreviewState, PaneTimelineStatus, and pane interaction
functions (adaptive_magnetic_field_cells, adaptive_dock_strength_bps,
edge_fling_projection, blend_rect, build_preview_state_from_candidates,
dynamic_live_reflow_threshold_bps, dynamic_preview_switch_advantage_bps,
and related constants) from ftui-showcase-wasm/src/runner_core.rs into
new ftui-demo-showcase::pane_interaction module.

Update import paths in runner_core.rs and wasm.rs to reference the new
shared module. Slightly adjust function signatures (e.g.,
adaptive_magnetic_field_cells now takes explicit parameter).

Update beads bd-2vr05.13.2 to in_progress, assigned to FrostyGate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.12.6","title":"task(test): execute full release rehearsal E2E scripts with telemetry/logging signoff","description":"Background
- Release track had planning artifacts but no explicit full-system rehearsal gate.

Goal
- Add a release rehearsal task that runs end-to-end user workflows through staged rollout logic with strict telemetry validation.

Scope
- End-to-end scripts that exercise remote attach, scrollback, selection, search, accessibility modes, and fallback rendering.
- Deterministic logging requirements: JSONL trace segments, benchmark outputs, rollout checkpoint events, rollback trigger evidence.
- Produce signoff packet linking rehearsal evidence to final go/no-go checklist.

Why This Matters
- Converts readiness theory into demonstrated release confidence.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This task must add or update focused unit tests for core logic touched.
- This task must link to at least one end-to-end script path that validates integrated behavior.
- Validation output must include structured logs detailed enough for deterministic postmortem analysis.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Comprehensive unit tests cover core logic and edge cases for this scope.
- Deterministic E2E scripts validate real user workflows relevant to this scope.
- Structured logs include timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for replay/debugging.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Comprehensive unit tests are implemented for the scoped subsystem, including edge cases and regression vectors.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are implemented for the scoped workflows and runnable in CI.
- Test execution emits detailed structured logs (JSONL) including timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context.
- At least one deliberate failure injection scenario is covered and produces actionable diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:07.689025233Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:16.114270191Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","frankenterm","release","rollout","telemetry","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.12.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.11.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:13.836846289Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.12.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:07.689025233Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.12.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.12.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:12.275031304Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.12.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.12.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:12.654309045Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.12.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.12.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:13.047206507Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.12.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:16.114147492Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.12.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:03:13.448502229Z","created_by":"HazyFalcon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13","title":"feature(frankenterm-js): advanced host API parity (events, parser hooks, markers, runtime options)","description":"Background
- Existing beads cover remote mode, attach lifecycle, selection, accessibility, renderer, conformance, security, and SDK surface.
- One critical parity area is still implicit instead of explicit: host-facing advanced API parity that real xterm.js embedders rely on (event lifecycle, parser hooks, marker/decoration primitives, and runtime option mutation).

Goal
- Deliver a first-class FrankenTermJS advanced host API track that matches or exceeds xterm.js practical capabilities while preserving FrankenTerm principles (Rust-first determinism, bounded resources, and explicit capability flags).

Scope
- Typed event API with deterministic ordering and backpressure-safe listener lifecycle.
- Parser hook APIs for CSI/OSC/DCS/ESC with explicit policy and quotas.
- Marker and decoration primitives (including viewport anchors) suitable for terminal-integrated UX.
- Runtime option and theme mutation APIs with validation and deterministic reconfigure semantics.
- Migration-grade compatibility mapping and evidence.

Why This Matters
- Without explicit advanced host API parity, complex xterm.js integrators cannot migrate cleanly even if core rendering/perf is superior.
- Making this a first-class feature avoids hidden scope and prevents regressions in release readiness.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- This feature track must ship with comprehensive unit-level API and lifecycle tests.
- This feature track must ship with deterministic browser E2E fixtures that validate integration behavior.
- Validation must emit structured logs sufficient for replay-grade debugging.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Advanced host API parity scope is fully decomposed into implementation and validation tasks.
- Unit + E2E + structured-log quality gates are defined and release-blocking.
- Compatibility evidence is available for migration and release scorecards.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Advanced host API parity is implemented across event lifecycle APIs, parser hooks, marker/decoration primitives, and runtime option mutation/theming.
- Comprehensive unit tests cover API contracts, ordering/lifecycle invariants, resource policy enforcement, and regression edges.
- Deterministic browser E2E scripts validate advanced API behavior across supported engines and degraded-capability scenarios.
- Validation emits structured logs (JSONL) with sequence IDs, hook traces, option diffs, correlation IDs, and failure context suitable for replay.
- Compatibility evidence is integrated into release scorecard and migration documentation.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:05.474877520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:23:35.401969703Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","events","frankenterm","markers","parity","parser","sdk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:05.474877520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:06.263774517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:06.642577073Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:07.037688755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","title":"task(api-events): define deterministic host event taxonomy and ordering contract","description":"Background
- Integrators need deterministic, typed lifecycle events comparable to xterm.js (`onData`, `onBinary`, `onResize`, render/selection/title/bell notifications) but with stricter ordering guarantees.

Goal
- Define a canonical FrankenTermJS host event taxonomy, ordering contract, and failure/backpressure behavior.

Scope
- Enumerate supported events and payload schemas at Rust/JS boundary.
- Specify ordering guarantees across feed/render/reply/selection paths.
- Define listener lifecycle, disposal, and bounded buffering policy.
- Specify structured diagnostic fields per event class.

Why This Matters
- Clear event contracts prevent integration drift and eliminate undocumented behavior dependencies.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Add unit tests for event schema validation and ordering invariants.
- Add E2E fixtures that assert event sequencing under resize, burst input, and mode transitions.
- Emit structured event logs with correlation IDs for replay.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Event taxonomy + ordering contract is documented and implemented as enforceable tests.
- Deterministic E2E sequencing checks pass across target browsers.
- Structured logs include event type, sequence number, timestamp, and correlation context.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:44.642323958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T18:30:32.267446554Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T18:30:32.267428580Z","close_reason":"Completed deterministic host-event taxonomy/ordering contract with Node+WASM E2E fixture and structured sequence logs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","events","frankenterm","sdk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:44.642323958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:07.436385548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","title":"task(api-events): implement typed event emitter lifecycle with bounded delivery semantics","description":"Background
- Event contract design must be realized with a performant boundary implementation that remains deterministic under load.

Goal
- Implement event emission and listener lifecycle APIs with bounded buffering and explicit drop/backpressure semantics.

Scope
- Implement typed event emitters and subscription handles in WASM/JS boundary.
- Enforce stable ordering from the contract under concurrent feed/render updates.
- Implement listener disposal and memory-safe cleanup guarantees.
- Instrument event pipeline with structured tracing fields.

Why This Matters
- Reliable event delivery is mandatory for production embeddings and analytics hooks.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Unit tests validate ordering, listener disposal, and bounded queue semantics.
- E2E tests validate event behavior in realistic browser embedding scenarios.
- Structured logs capture queue depth, drop counters, and sequence continuity.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Event emitter implementation satisfies contract invariants under stress.
- Browser E2E scenarios confirm deterministic behavior and no leaks after listener disposal.
- Structured logs are emitted for all event pipeline anomalies and steady-state telemetry.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.037058054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:22:14.936756500Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","events","frankenterm","observability","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:08.211291673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.037058054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:07.822786045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Scope

 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","title":"task(parser): implement CSI/OSC/DCS/ESC hook APIs with quotas and capability gating","description":"Background
- Advanced terminal apps rely on parser hook extension points (CSI/OSC/DCS/ESC handlers) for domain-specific control flows.

Goal
- Implement explicit parser hook registration APIs with deterministic execution order, quotas, and policy controls.

Scope
- Provide registration/deregistration for supported control-sequence classes.
- Enforce ordering, fallback behavior, and error isolation.
- Add resource quotas/timeouts to prevent host abuse.
- Integrate capability flags so unsupported hooks fail predictably.

Why This Matters
- Parser hooks are a key xterm.js differentiator; we need parity without unbounded risk.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Unit tests cover hook registration lifecycle, ordering, and isolation/fallback behavior.
- E2E scenarios validate host-defined handlers against real terminal traffic.
- Structured logs capture hook invocation traces, latency, and policy rejections.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Hook APIs support CSI/OSC/DCS/ESC classes with deterministic behavior.
- Resource policy enforcement is validated by tests, including adversarial cases.
- Structured logging provides replay-grade hook execution traces.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FrostyGate","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.442265142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T14:56:45.449790873Z","closed_at":"2026-02-16T14:56:45.449767009Z","close_reason":"Completed parser hook registration/deregistration for CSI/OSC/DCS/ESC with deterministic ordering, capability gating, quotas/timeouts, panic isolation fallback, and structured replay trace events in ftui-extras parser.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","frankenterm","parser","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:08.981452926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.442265142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:08.593585954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","title":"task(markers): implement marker and decoration primitives with viewport-stable anchors","description":"Background
- Marker/decoration primitives are needed for IDE-like overlays, diagnostics, and workflow UX parity.

Goal
- Implement marker + decoration primitives tied to viewport/scrollback model with deterministic anchor behavior.

Scope
- Marker creation/lifecycle APIs with stable anchor semantics across resize and scrollback compaction.
- Decoration model (inline/line/range) with renderer-safe invalidation behavior.
- Integration points for selection/search overlays without race conditions.
- Structured diagnostics for marker/deco churn and invalidation.

Why This Matters
- Rich terminal UX requires first-class annotation primitives; this is a common migration blocker.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Unit tests for anchor invariants under scroll, resize, wrap, and compaction.
- E2E tests for marker/deco behavior across interactive workflows.
- Structured logs track anchor moves, invalidations, and render synchronization.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Marker/decoration APIs are stable and deterministic under viewport mutations.
- E2E parity scenarios validate overlay correctness under churn.
- Logging surfaces enough context to debug anchor drift or stale decorations.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.856362540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:22:15.834348970Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","frankenterm","markers","renderer","scrollback"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:45.856362540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:09.377193365Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:09.777374566Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:10.176457101Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","title":"task(options): implement runtime option/theming mutation API with atomic validation","description":"Background
- Integrators need runtime option updates (theme, cursor, behavior flags, perf controls) without re-instantiation.

Goal
- Implement runtime option mutation/theming API with strict validation and deterministic hot-reconfigure behavior.

Scope
- Typed option schema with safe defaults and capability/profile gating.
- Atomic update semantics and rollback on invalid payloads.
- Renderer + engine synchronization for option changes.
- Structured diagnostics for applied/rejected updates.

Why This Matters
- Runtime reconfiguration is central to production embeddings and migration parity.

## Comprehensive Validation Hooks
- Unit tests for option schema validation, atomic updates, and rollback behavior.
- E2E tests for live reconfigure across backends and accessibility modes.
- Structured logs record option diffs, validation outcomes, and applied capability profile.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runtime option API supports deterministic hot updates with explicit validation failures.
- Cross-backend E2E scenarios confirm consistent behavior after live reconfiguration.
- Structured logs provide complete option-change auditability.","acceptance_criteria":"

## Mandatory Cross-Cut Quality Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for the implementation surface and edge-case regressions touched by this task.
- Deterministic end-to-end scripts are required for workflows materially affected by this task, and must run in CI.
- Validation output must include detailed structured logs (JSONL) with timestamps, correlation IDs, and failure context sufficient for deterministic replay and triage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:46.256457841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:22:16.295417175Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","frankenterm","renderer","sdk","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:20:46.256457841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.13.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:10.576838396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:11.370001954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vr05.13.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vr05.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:21:10.972463790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
